steps.title: Translate 'twice the value of x'
steps.content: The phrase twice the value of x means $2x$.

steps.title: Translate '49 more than'
steps.content: 49 more than a quantity means adding 49 to it. So $y$ equals $2x$ plus 49.

choiceAnalysis:
• Choice A — '$y=2x+49$': Twice $x$ is $2x$; 49 more than that gives $2x+49$. ✓
  • Choice B — '$y=49x+2$': Swaps the roles of 2 and 49 — this would mean 49 times $x$, plus 2. ✗
  • Choice C — '$y=51x+2$': Incorrectly adds 49 and 2 into the coefficient of $x$ rather than treating them as separate operations. ✗
  • Choice D — '$y=51x+49$': Combines both errors — coefficient $51$ and addend $49$. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
